mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payroll employment drives consumer income and spending, supporting corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If Rank IC of PAYEMS changes on forward 20-day returns is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate indicates weakening labor demand and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger activates, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate is low and retail sales are strong, monetary accommodation combines with robust demand to compress equity risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint signal does not yield a mean forward 20-day return greater than the overall sample mean (t-test p>0.05), the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decelerates due to capacity constraints, signaling an imminent economic peak and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the negative return frequency following trigger is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p<0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand, which directly fuels corporate revenues and equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS changes and 20-day forward returns is not statistically positive (p>0.05) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.2 million units indicate strong construction activity, which has positive spillovers to employment and durable goods, supporting equity returns.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return when trigger is active is not greater than the unconditional average (difference <0), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When employment growth is strong but the Fed is raising interest rates, the policy tightening eventually chokes off the expansion, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150,000 and FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp from 3 months ago
failure_condition: If the joint trigger does not precede a negative 5-day return more than 60% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, real interest rates are lower and valuations are supported, leading to positive equity returns over the month.
failure_condition: If the mean forward 20-day return during low inflation periods is not significantly higher than during high inflation periods (t-test p>0.05), reject.
